description: This figure presents a dual-axis line plot showing the composition dependence of elastic parameters for Fe-Al alloys with aluminium content ranging from 0 to 40 at.%. The left y-axis displays stiffness constants in GPa, while the right y-axis shows the anisotropy factor A (dimensionless). Three distinct curves are plotted: C44 (solid circles, ●) increases gradually from ~118 GPa to a peak near 130 GPa around 28-30% Al before slightly decreasing; (C11-C12)/2 (solid squares, ■) decreases from ~48 GPa to a minimum of ~20 GPa near 25% Al then modestly recovers; and the Zener anisotropy factor A (open triangles, △, calculated as A = 2C44/(C11-C12)) exhibits a sharp maximum reaching ~6.7 near the Fe3Al composition (~25-28% Al), coinciding with the onset of long-range DO3/B2 order. The caption states data is taken from Refs. [22,23]. The figure supports the core conclusion that Fe3Al alloys possess extremely high elastic anisotropy arising from simultaneous C44 increase and (C11-C12)/2 decrease, which correlates with the composition range where dislocation configurations change from single dislocations to superdislocations and where peak work hardening occurs.
